tasks.description: Generate JSDoc for a single exported function with clear signature

scoring.5: Summary, all params (to, subject, body, options with sub-properties), return type with both status values, both ValidationErrors documented with trigger conditions
scoring.3: Summary and params documented but missing error conditions or options sub-properties
scoring.1: Only summary and basic param types

scoring.5: At least one basic usage example and one with options; shows expected return value; uses realistic email data
scoring.3: One example but missing options usage or expected output

scoring.5: Proper JSDoc tags (@param, @returns, @throws, @example); consistent formatting; options sub-properties documented with @property or nested description
scoring.3: Correct tags but inconsistent formatting

tasks.description: Generate a changelog entry from a set of commit messages
tasks.input:
Generate a Keep a Changelog entry for version 2.3.0 (release date: 2024-09-20) from these commit messages:

- feat: add batch import endpoint for CSV files (#312)
- feat: add export to PDF functionality (#318)
- fix: correct timezone handling in scheduled reports (#305)
- fix: prevent duplicate webhook deliveries on retry (#309)
- refactor: extract validation logic into shared middleware
- chore: update CI pipeline to Node 20
- fix: resolve memory leak in long-running WebSocket connections (#315)
- feat(api): add pagination support to /events endpoint (#320)
- deprecated: mark /v1/reports endpoint for removal in 3.0 (#322)
- security: patch SSRF vulnerability in URL preview feature (CVE-2024-98765)

scoring.5: Correctly maps all commits to Keep a Changelog categories (Added, Changed, Fixed, Deprecated, Security); excludes non-user-facing changes (refactor, chore)
scoring.3: Most commits correctly categorized but includes internal changes or miscategorizes some
scoring.1: Categories present but many commits misplaced
scoring.0: No categorization or raw commit dump

scoring.5: Entries rewritten in imperative mood, human-readable, with issue/PR references preserved; security entry includes CVE reference
scoring.3: Entries are readable but some are just cleaned-up commit messages
scoring.1: Raw commit messages copied without rewriting

scoring.5: Follows Keep a Changelog format exactly: version heading with date, category headings, bulleted entries, comparison link placeholder
scoring.3: Mostly correct format but missing date or comparison link

tasks.description: Generate a README Quick Start section for a simple npm package
tasks.input:
Generate a complete README for this npm package based on the following information:

- Package name: @acme/rate-limiter
- Description: A lightweight, in-memory rate limiter for Node.js applications with sliding window support
- Install: npm/yarn/pnpm
- Main export: `createLimiter(options)` returns a limiter instance
- Options: { maxRequests: number (default 100), windowMs: number (default 60000), keyGenerator?: (req) => string }
- Methods: `limiter.check(key): { allowed: boolean, remaining: number, resetAt: Date }`, `limiter.reset(key): void`
- Requires Node.js >= 18
- License: MIT
- Author: Acme Corp

scoring.5: Complete README with title, description, installation (all 3 managers), quick start, API reference, configuration table, license; proper heading hierarchy
scoring.3: Most sections present but missing one of install/quick-start/API
scoring.1: Only partial sections; no clear structure

scoring.5: Copy-pasteable example under 10 lines showing createLimiter + check; includes import, setup, and usage with realistic data and expected output
scoring.3: Example present but not immediately runnable or missing expected output

scoring.5: All options and methods documented in tables with types, defaults, and descriptions; return types clearly specified
scoring.3: API documented but missing some defaults or type information

tasks.description: Generate OpenAPI 3.x specification from Express route handlers

scoring.5: All 5 endpoints documented with correct HTTP methods, paths, and operationIds; authentication requirements (authenticate vs optionalAuth) correctly mapped to security schemes
scoring.3: All endpoints present but authentication requirements not differentiated
scoring.1: Some endpoints missing or incorrect methods

scoring.5: Request bodies and response schemas fully defined with correct types, required fields, constraints (minLength, maxLength, enum), and defaults; pagination response schema included; Error schema defined
scoring.3: Schemas present but missing constraints or defaults

scoring.5: All response codes documented (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 403, 404) with appropriate schemas and examples for each endpoint
scoring.3: Success responses documented but error responses incomplete

scoring.5: Request and response examples with realistic data for each endpoint; includes cURL examples
scoring.3: Some examples present but not for all endpoints

tasks.description: Document an undocumented class with inheritance and complex method signatures

scoring.5: Both classes have class-level TSDoc: purpose, generic constraints explained, design pattern noted (Repository), inheritance relationship documented; abstract method contract explained
scoring.3: Class-level docs present but missing generic explanation or inheritance notes

scoring.5: All 10 methods fully documented: summary, params with semantic meaning, return values including null/false cases, all thrown errors with conditions; abstract validate contract specified
scoring.3: Most methods documented but some missing error conditions or null return cases
scoring.1: Only basic summaries without param/return details

scoring.5: Generic type parameter T documented with @typeParam; constraint `{ id: string }` explained; Partial<T>, Omit<T, 'id'> utility types explained in context; OrderStatus type referenced
scoring.3: Generics mentioned but not fully explained

scoring.5: Usage examples for both BaseRepository (via OrderRepository) and OrderRepository-specific methods; shows creation, querying, updating, error handling
scoring.3: Some examples but not covering key methods

tasks.description: Update stale documentation to match refactored code

scoring.5: Identifies all inaccuracies: (1) param changed from username to identifier with union type, (2) second param restructured from boolean to options object, (3) new includePermissions and cache options, (4) returns null instead of throwing NotFoundError, (5) caching behavior added
scoring.3: Identifies 3-4 inaccuracies

scoring.5: Complete updated JSDoc reflecting all current behavior: union type identifier, full options object with all 3 properties and defaults, null return case, caching behavior, no false NotFoundError @throws
scoring.3: Updated docs mostly correct but missing some new behavior
scoring.1: Partially updated; still contains stale information

scoring.5: Documents caching behavior (TTL, key strategy), explains identifier union type usage (string ID vs email object), describes what includeProfile and includePermissions add to the response
scoring.3: Some new behavior documented but caching or union type not fully explained

scoring.5: Examples showing both identifier forms (string and {email}), with and without options, demonstrating null return
scoring.3: One example showing basic usage

tasks.description: Generate documentation for a configuration-heavy module with many options

scoring.5: Complete configuration table with all options: name, type, default, required/optional, description; including nested hooks properties; constraints documented (file output requires filePath)
scoring.3: Most options documented but missing some defaults or nested properties

scoring.5: LoggerConfig interface, createLogger function, and all Logger methods fully documented with proper tags; Partial<LoggerConfig> explained; redactPaths behavior described
scoring.3: Most symbols documented but some methods or properties lack detail

scoring.5: Multiple examples: basic setup, file output with rotation, pretty format for development, custom levels, child loggers, redaction usage; each example is runnable
scoring.3: 2-3 examples covering basic and one advanced scenario

scoring.5: README section with configuration table, quick start, example configurations for common use cases (development, production, testing)
scoring.3: README section present but not organized for different use cases

tasks.description: Generate a complete documentation suite for an undocumented microservice

scoring.5: Full README with: project description, prerequisites, installation, environment configuration (API keys, database URL, etc.), API overview with endpoint table, authentication section, error handling conventions, link to full API spec
scoring.3: README covers basics but missing configuration or error handling sections
scoring.1: Minimal README with just endpoint listing

scoring.5: Complete OpenAPI 3.x spec with all 5 endpoints, correct schemas (PaymentIntent, Refund, Error, PaginatedResponse), all response codes, Idempotency-Key header, authentication scheme, request/response examples, proper use of $ref for shared schemas
scoring.3: All endpoints documented but schemas incomplete or missing some response codes
scoring.1: Partial endpoint coverage or incorrect schemas
scoring.0: No OpenAPI spec or fundamentally incorrect

scoring.5: Keep a Changelog format for v1.3.0; commits correctly categorized (Added, Fixed, Security); entries rewritten for user clarity; internal changes excluded; issue refs preserved
scoring.3: Correct format but some miscategorization or commit messages not rewritten
scoring.1: Raw commit list or wrong format

scoring.5: Schemas, endpoint names, and field descriptions are consistent across README, OpenAPI spec, and changelog; no contradictions between documents
scoring.3: Mostly consistent but minor discrepancies

scoring.5: Payment processing concepts correctly applied: idempotency for creation, partial capture semantics, refund limits, ISO 4217 currency codes, amounts in cents
scoring.3: Basic payment concepts correct but some nuances missed

tasks.description: Generate documentation for a complex event-driven system with multiple interacting components

scoring.5: Documents the event flow: Event emitted -> Router matches rules -> Template rendered -> Channel delivers; explains component relationships; includes flow diagram or clear textual description of the pipeline
scoring.3: Components individually documented but interaction flow not clear

scoring.5: Every class, interface, type, and method documented: NotificationService (all 6 methods), NotificationRouter (3 methods), TemplateEngine (3 methods), NotificationChannel interface, all event types, RoutingRule fields; @typeParam and generics explained
scoring.3: Main classes documented but some methods or types missing

scoring.5: Full configuration guide: NotificationConfig options, channel setup (API keys, credentials), routing rules creation, template directory structure, user preferences schema; includes example configuration for common setups
scoring.3: Basic configuration covered but missing channel-specific setup

scoring.5: End-to-end example: creating service, configuring channels, defining routing rules, emitting events, handling delivery callbacks; plus individual examples for each component; error handling demonstrated
scoring.3: Basic setup and emit example but missing component-level examples

scoring.5: Documents: channel health check failures, delivery retries, throttle behavior, template validation errors, graceful shutdown behavior, bounced notification handling
scoring.3: Some error cases documented but missing edge cases

tasks.description: Generate documentation that bridges code-level and user-level understanding for a CLI tool

scoring.5: Complete CLI documentation: installation, all 6 commands with flags and descriptions, workflow examples (creating first migration, running up, checking status, rolling back), configuration file example, prerequisites (database setup)
scoring.3: Commands listed but missing workflow examples or configuration guide

scoring.5: Full Migrator class API: constructor with config documentation, all 8 methods with params/returns/errors, MigratorConfig interface with all options and defaults, MigrationResult and MigrationStatus types described; distributed locking behavior explained
scoring.3: Main methods documented but missing config details or lock behavior

scoring.5: Clear guide on: naming convention (timestamp_name), file structure (Migration interface), up/down contract (idempotency, reversibility), DatabaseClient methods available, examples for common operations (create table, add column, create index, data migration)
scoring.3: Basic format explained but missing common operation examples
scoring.1: Only the interface shown without guidance

scoring.5: Documents: dry-run behavior, transaction modes and when to use each, distributed locking for multi-instance deployments, reset safety (--confirm flag), what happens on migration failure, partial migration recovery
scoring.3: Some safety topics covered

scoring.5: Real-world examples throughout: CLI command sequences for common workflows, programmatic usage with error handling, 3+ migration file examples (DDL, data migration, index), configuration for different environments (dev, staging, prod)
scoring.3: Some examples present but not covering real workflows
